threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You order a flat white and don’t know the barista's name, but they already remember yours from two visits ago.
Caught the 59 last night; two guys arguing about who won a fight with a pigeon outside Brixton Station. Only in London, right?

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: Sitting at the corner café, I watched a guy chase his runaway kebab down the street. He caught it, then shared half with a stray cat. That’s Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin can you find a bus stop where a DJ sets up his turntables at 6 PM. The whole station turns into a mini rave while you wait!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: A true local knows the best spot to dodge the Civic Center BART chaos—while newcomers still gawk at the smoke and sirens.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a Muni bus today, driver paused mid-ride to let a couple of street performers finish their act. Only in the TL would that happen.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think the Sagrada Família is the heart of Barcelona. Real locals know that the true vibe is in the winding streets and small plazas here, like Plaça de la Vila.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always spot them fumbling with the metro map, eagerly asking locals for directions but still managing to end up on the wrong line.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: When you still refer to Dalston Junction as the place with the big green train station sign rather than just ‘Dalston’ like everyone else.
Caught a night bus from Brixton to central; a guy serenaded the whole back row with 80s ballads. Best late-night ride I've had in ages!

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I found myself waiting at the bus stop, listening to an impromptu rap battle between two kids on scooters. Only in Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin do you get a DJ spinning vinyl at the weekly flea market while you sip on craft kombucha. Flea market vibes with a rave twist!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Only locals know to avoid Civic Center BART when it’s rainy—smoke, delays, and a dog on the tracks are just part of the commute.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 5 today, stopped for a good 10 minutes because of a man giving a poetry reading on the steps of City Hall. Classic SF.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists often think Gràcia is just for hip cafés, but the real charm is in the hidden plazas where locals gather for a game of petanca.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a newbie by the way they still stop for every street musician, pulling out their phone to record. Locals just stroll by.
